questions.text: What is the legal punishment for Zina (adultery/fornication) mentioned in Verse 2?
questions.answer: One hundred lashes for each of them (if unmarried).

questions.text: What is the punishment for those who accuse chaste women without bringing four witnesses?
questions.answer: Eighty lashes, and their testimony is never to be accepted again.

questions.text: What is 'Li'an' (Mutual Cursing)?
questions.answer: A legal process where a husband accuses his wife of adultery without witnesses; they testify four times by Allah, with a fifth oath invoking Allah's curse/wrath.

questions.text: Which major historical event is referred to as 'Al-Ifk' (The Slander)?
questions.answer: The false accusation against Aisha (RA), the wife of the Prophet (SAW).

questions.text: What was the advice to the believers who heard the slander against Aisha?
questions.answer: They should have thought well of their own people and said, 'This is a clear falsehood'.

questions.text: What is the etiquette of entering homes other than your own?
questions.answer: Do not enter until you have asked permission and greeted the inhabitants.

questions.text: What should one do if told to 'go back' at someone's door?
questions.answer: Go back, for it is purer for you.

questions.text: What is the command to believing men and women regarding their gaze?
questions.answer: To lower their gaze and guard their private parts.

questions.text: What is the 'Khimar' mentioned in Verse 31?
questions.answer: A headcovering that women should wrap over their chests (Juyubihinna).

questions.text: Describe the famous 'Verse of Light' (Ayat al-Nur).
questions.answer: Allah is the Light of the heavens and earth. His light is like a niche containing a lamp in a glass, the glass like a pearly star, lit from a blessed olive tree.

questions.text: What do the deeds of disbelievers resemble in Verse 39?
questions.answer: A mirage in a lowland which a thirsty person thinks is water until he reaches it and finds nothing.

questions.text: What parable describes the 'darkness' of the disbelievers' state?
questions.answer: Darkness in a deep sea, covered by waves upon waves, covered by clouds—darkness upon darkness.

questions.text: What do the birds do in the sky according to Verse 41?
questions.answer: They spread their wings in flight, and each knows its prayer and its way of exalting Allah.

questions.text: What will testify against the people on the Day of Judgment (Verse 24)?
questions.answer: Their tongues, their hands, and their feet.

questions.text: What are the 'three times of privacy' when children/servants must ask permission to enter?
questions.answer: Before the Fajr prayer, when you take off your clothes at noon, and after the Isha prayer.

questions.text: Is there any blame for the blind, the x-lame, or the sick regarding eating at others' houses?
questions.answer: No, there is no restriction on them eating from their own houses or houses of relatives/friends.

questions.text: What is the promise to those who believe and do righteous deeds regarding 'Istikhlaf'?
questions.answer: Allah will surely grant them succession (authority) upon the earth as He granted those before them.

questions.text: How should the believers address the Messenger of Allah?
questions.answer: They should not make the calling of the Messenger among them like the calling of one of them to another.

questions.text: What does Allah say about the 'houses' where His name is mentioned?
questions.answer: They are houses which Allah has permitted to be raised and where His name is celebrated.

questions.text: Who are the 'men' described in Verse 37?
questions.answer: Those whom neither commerce nor sale distracts from the remembrance of Allah, establishing prayer, and giving Zakat.
